description: From the manufacturer OVER 25 YEARS OF RODENT CONTROL MADE EASY! Catch & Kill: We offer a wide selection of conventional spring traps with a powerful snap and hold. Our Fresh 'n Tasty range is ready-baited with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant - a poison-free formula proven to dramatically increase catch rate. Pet-safe and easy-set options are also available making The Big Cheese Catch & Kill range suitable for use in most areas of the household. Bait & Kill: Our Home Choice range offers a bait for every situation, and with 50% LESS POISON than previous formulations, it is the ideal choice for use in and around the home. The range includes damp proof wax blocks suitable for use in all weather conditions, chew thru grain bait sachets for easy bait placement, and highly palatable fresh pasta bait recommended for food preparation areas. Follow our easy on-pack icons to choose the most effective bait for your situation. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality cage traps and ready-baited mouse traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Easy-set mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced ultrasonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Plus a new selection of low-cost consumables designed to help defend homes from rodent infestations. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Ready-to-use mouse trap - twin pack Rodent control made easy! When mice and rats move in, it's time to bring in The Big Cheese! Choose from a comprehensive range of tried and tested traps, deterrents and baits within 4 straight-forward methods of control. Fresh Baited Mouse Traps -2pk from The Big Cheese Fresh baited, tested and proven to catch more rodents. Fresh Baited Mouse Traps from The Big Cheese have a solid 1cm wooden base, covered with a plastic sleeve making them easy to wipe clean and suitable for indoor household use and outdoor garden use. Fresh baited traps come ready baited with delicious-to-rodents Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse and Rat Attractant bait for a quicker catch. The powerful spring mechanism ensures rodent pests are dispatched in a fast, humane way. Powerful, corrosion resistant spring mechanism. Sleeve block for easy cleaning. Solid 1cm wooden base suitable for repeated use without breaking. Suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Refill with Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant (purchased separately). Twin pack. Part of the comprehensive range of rodent control products from The Big Cheese. Product Highlights Poison-free Fresh 'n Tasty bait Poison free and proven to attract rodents with an incredible catch rate compared to scented pedals and other traditional attractants. Reusable Plastic sleeve is wipe clean to prevent degrading wooden platform. Powerful Robust trap mechanism keeps on performing. Strong 1cm wooden base to withstand the powerful snap and hold force of the trap. Powerful spring mechanism for a sure kill. The corrosion-resistant spring maintains strength and performance use after use ensuring an instant humane kill. Indoor and outdoor use Suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Mice Mice are a highly adaptive and invasive species and they’re able to populate a variety of ecosystems. Agricultural fields, buildings, and boats are all viable habitats. In more temperate climates there will be even more possible nest sites. Swimming, climbing, jumping proficiency, and the two incisors on their upper jaw, all make for a formidable predator wherever they reside. However, despite all of these amazing abilities, the house mouse chooses to reside, as its name would indicate, in a home. House mice ( Mus Musculus Domesticus ) are a highly adaptive member of the genus Mus (mouse), yet for some reason they choose to live in the least demanding environment, a human home. They are attracted by the continuous flow of food and warmth, ideal for nest sites. The offer of food and shelter is so tempting that many choose to stay and breed year-round. Diet As one can imagine, the palate of a house mouse is highly adaptable since every habitat they migrate through will hold different opportunities for feeding. Outside dwellers will eat insects and a lot of grain, however, inside dwelling mice are practically omnivorous and, just like brown rats, will consume 10-15% of their body weight each day in just about anything we throw away. Most house mice will nibble on multiple different food sources to obtain their required nutrition, up to 20 times each night, since they’re nocturnal. A large proportion of a mouse’s 3g daily intake will be grain, namely cereal, favouring high-energy, man-made foods. Studies have proven that the levels of aggression in both rats and mice can be attributed to this high fat ‘human’ diet they have both been on for so long. These foods increase oestrogen levels and results in a shorter temper and longer periods of aggression. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
